Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Normative Process Rules 1 Normal flow sufficient inventory Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order 2 Cancellation flow insufficient inventory Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order 3 Key constraints Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Close order should be the final activity in all scenarios Process always begins with Receive order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close This matches the normal flow exactly No violations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close Package items occurs BEFORE Authorize payment Violation The description states Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping More importantly it says Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging So packaging should happen AFTER authorization not before This is a clear violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close Multiple violations 1 Ship order should never occur after a cancellation The description explicitly states Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation 2 If inventory is insufficient which led to cancellation why was payment authorized The normative process says authorization should only happen when inventory is sufficient Actually rereading the description Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging so it can occur after check inventory but should be in the sufficient inventory path Since the order was canceled it implies inventory was insufficient so authorization should not have happened 3 Also after cancellation the next step should be Close order not Ship order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive Check Cancel Close This matches the cancellation flow exactly No violations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Violation Ship order occurs before Package items The description states items must be packaged before shipping Missing Package items activity and Ship order happens before packaging Also the description clearly states the order should be Authorize Package Ship

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me also consider whether there are any other ambiguities The description mentions that the exact nature of these sequences and the conditions they imply may contain some room for interpretation but the rules are quite clear 1 Package must come after Authorize 2 Ship must come after Package 3 Ship must NOT come after Cancel 4 Close must be last 5 Receive must be first 6 Check must be after Receive and before any other decisions Let me re verify my analysis Case 1 Compliant Case 2 Package before Authorize violates Package items should only occur after authorization Case 3 Ship after Cancel violates Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation also Authorize before Cancel is questionable Case 4 Compliant Case 5 Ship without Package missing Package step Ship should not occur before Package
